title: What would I do this week if I knew the money was easy?
subtitle: A journal prompt set for finding the decisions you are holding back because easy income still feels unreal.
excerpt: A journal prompt set from Week 5 of the MONEY program. Five prompts for identifying the specific choices you would make differently if easy income felt settled and real.
sourceNotes: Adapted from the Week 5 daily reflection prompts in The Money Journal: 12 Weeks to Peace, Freedom and Overflow (Rebecca J Page, 2025).

practice.whenToUse: Use when you want to find out what you are holding back because easy income still doesn't feel real enough to plan from.

content.text: A journal prompt set from Week 5 of the MONEY program. The interesting question is not what you would buy if you had more money: it is what you would do differently this week if you believed the easy income was already settled.
New to journalling as a practice? Read
How does money feel in my body this morning?
Write the honest answer: tight, open, neutral, worried. No elaboration needed yet.
What does 'easy income' mean to me right now?
Write the specific picture: what amount, what source, what schedule.
If easy income felt settled and real, what would I say yes to this week that I've been holding back on?
Write the specific decision: the investment, the hire, the commitment, the plan.
What am I doing this week from scarcity thinking rather than real information?
Write what you can identify. Where are you choosing the smaller option because the larger one still feels unsafe rather than unaffordable?
What is one thing I can do this week that reflects the belief that earning can be easy?
Write the action. Small is fine. The point is that it comes from the new belief rather than the old one.
Adapted from the Week 5 daily reflection prompts in The Money Journal: 12 Weeks to Peace, Freedom and Overflow (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
